topic: Quantum computing applications in cryptography post-2022
final_review:
The advent of quantum computing has significant implications for cryptography, particularly in the post-2022 era (Fernández‐Caramés, 2019; Fernández‐Caramés & Fraga‐Lamas, 2020). Researchers have been exploring the applications of quantum computing in cryptography, with a focus on developing quantum-resistant cryptosystems (Fernández‐Caramés, 2019; Alagic et al., 2022). The selection of cryptographic key sizes is crucial in this context, as highlighted by Lenstra and Verheul (2001). They emphasize the importance of choosing key sizes that are resistant to quantum computing attacks.

The development of post-quantum cryptography is closely tied to the advancement of quantum computing technology (Fernández‐Caramés & Fraga‐Lamas, 2020). Fernández‐Caramés and Fraga‐Lamas (2020) review the current state of blockchain cryptography and its resistance to quantum computing attacks. They highlight the need for post-quantum blockchain solutions that can withstand the computational power of quantum computers.

The integration of blockchain technology with cloud computing is another area of research that has implications for cryptography (Habib et al., 2022). Habib et al. (2022) discuss the benefits, challenges, and applications of blockchain technology, including its potential to enhance security and privacy. However, the security of blockchain technology is also a concern, as highlighted by Kumar et al. (2019) in their survey on smart grid metering networks. They identify security, privacy, and open research issues in this context.

The development of 6G technology is also relevant to the discussion of quantum computing applications in cryptography (Jiang et al., 2021; de Alwis et al., 2021). Jiang et al. (2021) provide a comprehensive survey of 6G technology, including its trends, applications, and requirements. De Alwis et al. (2021) also survey the frontiers of 6G, highlighting its potential to enable new applications and services.

In terms of future research directions, Tariq et al. (2023) provide a critical cybersecurity analysis and identify future research directions for the Internet of Things (IoT). They highlight the need for quantum-resistant cryptosystems and secure communication protocols in the IoT. Similarly, Fernández‐Caramés (2019) emphasizes the need for post-quantum IoT security and surveys the current state of quantum-resistant cryptosystems for the IoT.

In conclusion, the applications of quantum computing in cryptography are a rapidly evolving field, with significant implications for the development of post-quantum cryptography (Alagic et al., 2022; Fernández‐Caramés & Fraga‐Lamas, 2020). Further research is needed to develop quantum-resistant cryptosystems and secure communication protocols for the IoT and other applications (Tariq et al., 2023; Kumar et al., 2019).

References:
Alagic, G., Apon, D., & Cooper, D. A. (2022). Status report on the third round of the NIST Post-Quantum Cryptography Standardization process. https://doi.org/10.6028/nist.ir.8413
de Alwis, C., Kalla, A., & Pham, Q. V. (2021). Survey on 6G Frontiers: Trends, Applications, Requirements, Technologies and Future Research. https://doi.org/10.1109/ojcoms.2021.3071496
Fernández‐Caramés, T. M. (2019). From Pre-Quantum to Post-Quantum IoT Security: A Survey on Quantum-Resistant Cryptosystems for the Internet of Things. https://doi.org/10.1109/jiot.2019.2958788
Fernández‐Caramés, T. M., & Fraga‐Lamas, P. (2020). Towards Post-Quantum Blockchain: A Review on Blockchain Cryptography Resistant to Quantum Computing Attacks. https://doi.org/10.1109/access.2020.2968985
Habib, G., Sharma, S., & Ibrahim, S. (2022). Blockchain Technology: Benefits, Challenges, Applications, and Integration of Blockchain Technology with Cloud Computing. https://doi.org/10.3390/fi14110341
Jiang, W., Han, B., & Habibi, M. A. (2021). The Road Towards 6G: A Comprehensive Survey. https://doi.org/10.1109/ojcoms.2021.3057679
Kumar, P., Lin, Y., & Bai, G. (2019). Smart Grid Metering Networks: A Survey on Security, Privacy and Open Research Issues. https://doi.org/10.1109/comst.2019.2899354
Lenstra, A. K., & Verheul, E. R. (2001). Selecting Cryptographic Key Sizes. https://doi.org/10.1007/s00145-001-0009-4
Tariq, U., Ahmed, I., & Bashir, A. K. (2023). A Critical Cybersecurity Analysis and Future Research Directions for the Internet of Things: A Comprehensive Review. https://doi.org/10.3390/s23084117
